jobs.title: Construction Associate Environmental Specialist- Solar
jobs.description:
CA, United States \| Solar Group  

Mortenson is currently seeking an **Associate Environmental Specialist** with the ability to interact with a variety of staff at all levels in an ever\-changing environment, remaining flexible, resourceful, proactive, and efficient with a high level of professionalism and confidentiality. The Associate Environmental Specialist will support Mortenson's environmental management efforts for **Solar and Emerging Renewables** projects throughout North America, providing review of environmental documentation, and advising on environmental matters in a manner consistent with company policies and procedures.  

**Location: California, Fresno County****Travel:** This is a regional position that will be supporting projects in a specified region of the U.S. This is a **100% travel** position. Mortenson hires for a career and not a project.  

**RESPONSIBILITIES*** Implement and administer environmental program at each work site
* Support project management in developing and executing site\-specific environmental plans
* Participate in project planning and environmental risk analysis
* Review environmental submittals and attend preconstruction meetings with subcontractors
* Monitor and review trade partner work plans for environmental concerns
* Ensure compliance with environmental regulations and implement policies and procedures
* Coordinate and support environmental orientation and training programs
* Assist with onsite best management practices for environmental management
* Monitor implementation of environmental mitigation measures
* Conduct work site environmental inspections
* Monitor and record environmental data
* Document and report environmental violations and non\-compliance conditions
* Assist with incident analysis and response
* Provide environmental\-related information resources
* Maintain knowledge of environmental issues, regulations, and standards (US EPA, ASTM, etc.)
* Research environmental issues for project team members
* Interface with customer’s environmental personnel, consultants, and regulatory representatives
* Accompany environmental, health and safety, insurance, and regulatory personnel on site tours
* Ensure maintenance and inspection of environmental equipment
**QUALIFICATIONS*** Bachelor’s degree or higher required in Geology, Environmental Management, Environmental Science, Biology, or related field
* Previous related internship experience a plus
* Previous related internship experience a plus
* Current driver’s license
**A few benefits offered include:***(for Non\-Craft \& Non\-Union Craft working 25\+ hours / week)** Medical and prescription drug plans that includes no additional cost vision coverage
* Dental plan
* 401k retirement plan with a generous Mortenson match
* Paid time off, holidays, and other paid leaves
* Employer paid Life, AD\&D, and disability insurance
* No\-Cost mental health tool and concierge with extensive work\-life resources
* Tuition reimbursement
* Adoption Assistance
* Gym Membership Discount Program
**\*\*The base pay range for this role is $73,800 \- $92,300\.** (Actual range is higher for the following office locations: Denver, CO and Chicago, IL – 5%, Seattle, WA, and Portland, OR – 10%, Washington, D.C. – 12\.5%).  

Base pay is positioned within the range based on several factors including an individual’s knowledge, skills, and experience, with consideration given to internal equity. This position is eligible for Mortenson’s incentive plan.  

**\#LI\-GL1 \#LI\-onsite****Please make note:*** Visa sponsorship is not offered for this position.
* Our postings are typically open a minimum of 5 days and an average of 44 days.
**ABOUT MORTENSON**As a top builder, developer, and EPC (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction), our expertise spans markets like sports, renewable energy, data centers, healthcare, and more. We are builders at heart, working to ensure the built environment has a lasting positive impact.  

Let’s Redefine Possible®  

**Equal Employment Opportunity**Your uniqueness brings new and creative perspectives to the team. Mortenson is committed to providing equal opportunities of employment (EOE) to all individuals, regardless of your race, religion, gender, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, marital status or any other legally protected category.  

**Other Items to Note*** Mortenson reserves the right to hire any individual without legal or financial obligation on unwanted solicitations. *No agency emails, calls, or solicitations are accepted* without a valid agreement.
* Must be currently legally authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H1B status, 0\-1, TN, CPT, OPT, etc.). We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
**The Opportunity**  

Tetra Tech is looking for full\-time, Environmental Scientists to work out of our Rancho Cordova, CA or Oakland, CA offices.

**Position Summary**  

The selected candidate will provide technical support in the areas of environmental site assessment, brownfields, site investigation, and remediation activities. Team members are also responsible for the planning and oversight of operations and site health and safety. The work involves environmental consulting and site investigation services.

 **Essential Job Functions**  

The following duties are considered essential to the role. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions:  

* Multi\-media sampling
* Field audits and inspections
* Organize and review data
* Write and perform technical reviews of reports
* Plan field investigations
* Subcontractor oversight
* Perform Phase I and Phase II Environmental Site Assessments
* Brownfield assessments
* Site inspections and/or site investigations on sites with potentially hazardous substances
* Travel to lead and support environmental projects
**Why Tetra Tech**  

At Tetra Tech, we are Leading with Science to solve the world’s most complex challenges. Our industry\-leading experts in engineering and consulting are committed to driving positive change in communities around the world. For over 60 years, we have been at the forefront of innovation and sustainability. Today we stand as a market leader, offering cutting\-edge solutions in water, environment, energy, and international development. Our work has improved more than 625 million lives around the world.

**Your Impact**  

Join Tetra Tech to make a real difference. Our work leverages cutting\-edge technologies, advanced analytics, and the expertise of world\-class scientists and engineers to create meaningful change around the world. Discover your full potential – join us to advance your career while leaving a lasting legacy.

**Required Qualifications (Required)**  

* BS degree in environmental sciences or physical sciences (e.g., geology, biology hydrology, toxicology, health physics).
* 2\+ years of experience
* Must have prior experience in the environmental industry with demonstrated experience with practical field experience performing multi\-media sampling, including groundwater, soil, and soil gas
**Preferred Qualifications**  

* 40\-hour OSHA HAZWOPER training is a plus.
* Experience supporting remedial design, feasibility studies, hazardous waste, site investigations, site remediation, field sampling, air quality, ecological restoration, radiological issues, and/or water resources a plus
* Analytical skills related to qualitative and quantitative data
* Strong technical and non\-technical writing
* Information and data analysis
* Work on multiple projects teams simultaneously
* Proficiency with MS Office applications is required
**In Addition, we prefer you have/are:**  

* Well organized and strong attention to detail.
* Able to multi\-task
* Highly motivated, customer focused and work well in a team environment across multiple communication platforms.
* Excellent communication, responsiveness, and customer service skills.
* Ability to manage a heavy workload.
 **Work Environment/Environmental Factors (Required)**  

The work involves both office and field environments. Travel with overnight and weekends is required for some of our field efforts and anticipated to be throughout the continental west coast, southwest, and Hawaiian Islands. Potential travel nationwide. A physical capacity for working outdoors, often in isolated areas, is required.

 **Physical Requirements (Required)**  

The physical demands described here represent those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of the job. Reasonable accommodations may be made. You must be able to:  

* Work outdoors in all weather conditions. Climate ranges from extremely dry to extremely wet and from hot to cold. Rain, snow, wind, or dust may be encountered.
* Walk on uneven terrain. Terrain may be steep, uneven, rocky, and covered with vegetation.
* Pack heavy equipment and perform excavations using hand tools.
* Carry field equipment (35 to 40 pounds)
* Don personal protective gear as needed
* Drive on a regular basis.
* Pass a motor vehicle record background check and maintain a valid driver’s license and safe driving record is required.
**Additional Information (Required)**  

* Work schedule: Monday \- Friday
* Some field work requires working evenings and weekends and includes overnight stays when traveling.
* Work locations: Office work performed in our Rancho Cordova, CA or Oakland, CA office. Hybrid is available
* Travel requirements: 35%


This arrangement is subject to review and may change at the company’s discretion. **Life at Tetra Tech**  

The perks of working at Tetra Tech include:  

* Comprehensive and market\-competitive benefits.
* Merit\-based financial rewards.
* Flexibility and company\-wide commitment to work/life balance.
* Collaborative team atmosphere that values the contributions of all employees.
* Learning and development opportunities for ongoing professional growth.
  

Tetra Tech offers comprehensive benefit options that allow employees to design a plan based on individual needs and assists in planning for long\-term financial security. Available benefits include medical, dental and vision insurance, term life, disability, accidental death \& dismemberment coverage, Flexible Spending Account (FSA), Health Spending Account (HSA), time off with pay, 401k retirement plan, and an Employee Stock Purchase Plan.

  

The Salary Range for this position is $70,304 \- $80,000, depending on education, experience, skills, and location.


Health and safety play a vital role in our success. Tetra Tech’s employees work together to comply with all applicable health \& safety practices and protocols, including any public health o

jobs.title: Senior Remediation Construction Manager - Upstream
jobs.description:
**There’s no pledge more important than the one we make to look after our environment, and we’re committed to helping you do exactly that.**

Join us as we strive to ensure a sustainable future for our communities and the world we live in.  



Become part of our global network of skilled engineers, scientists, auditors, planners and environmental specialists, all working together to leave this world better than we found it.  



**Who are we looking for?**

Our Construction Management Group is growing! In response to the steady growth of our team in the West Region (Arizona, California, Washington) GHD is looking for a **Senior Construction Manager** with proven on time and budget delivery capabilities. You will provide GHD’s construction phase support services to new and existing clients on multiple simultaneous projects with a construction value of up to $250M each. This position can be located anywhere in the western US. Projects are anticipated in California between Fresno and northern Los Angeles. GHD has nearby offices in Fresno, San Luis Obispo, and Los Angeles.


**In an ever\-changing world, it requires creativity and innovation to stay ahead.**

We’re seeking the curious, those who are stimulated by fresh thinking and a desire to shape our communities in new, positive ways. As part of a truly global team, working on complex and rewarding projects, you'll be at the forefront of driving change.


**Our Hybrid Work Model** : Be part of the dynamic culture! Our workplace thrives on a hybrid model, combining in\-office collaboration and flexibility. Join us in the office or at a project site three days a week to contribute, connect and excel in our vibrant environment. Remote options may be available based on location of the applicant.
  

  

**Responsibilities of the position are but are not limited to:**

* **Construction Management:** for our environmental construction, site remediation, civil construction, and decommissioning projects with a key oil and gas client in the US West Region. Additional project opportunities exist in water (i.e. pumping stations, pipelines, treatment facilities, etc.), transportation (roads, trails, traffic improvements), as well as coastal, environmental, energy. In addition to leading during construction phase, contributions will include involvement in the planning, permitting, design, and procurement phases. This is an excellent opportunity for a qualified Construction Manager to be involved in all aspects of project planning and delivery.
* **Construction Project Controls:** Develop, deliver, and manage budget plans and schedules for construction projects. Monitor and perform budget progress and post\-calculations.
* **Insights and Reporting:** Contribute to the design and creation of reporting strategies and templates. Lead execution of complex reports, identifying and interpreting complex patterns and trends, and translating those insights into actionable recommendations. Communicate directly with client team.
* **Project Development:** collaborate with client development team, lead procurement efforts including proposal and interview preparation
* **Construction Advice:** Provide specialist professional advice relating to construction regulations and best practice with regard to constructability, cost evaluations, and sequencing for environmental and civil construction projects.
* **Knowledge Management:** Develop policies and processes, create best practices, set up case studies, and deliver internal knowledge\-sharing sessions to capture and share knowledge among colleagues on project/program outcomes and innovative practices in a particular area of expertise and to support the practice.
* **Personal Capability Building:** Act as subject matter expert in an area of construction management and execution for the team. Maintain external accreditations and in\-depth understanding of current and emerging external regulation and industry best practices through continuing professional development, attending conferences, and reading specialist media.
* **Bid Solicitation/Request for Proposal/Tender Analysis:** Conduct complex feasibility studies from technological, financial, and organizational perspectives on bid solicitation, including request for proposal (RFP) and request for tender (RFT) project requirements, and review specifications for tenders.
* **Operational Compliance:** Monitor and review performance and behaviors within area of responsibility to identify and resolve noncompliance with the organization's policies and relevant regulatory codes and codes of conduct.
* **Client \& Customer Management (External):** Manage important client relationships with guidance from senior colleagues or oversee relationship management with a group of more transactional clients and customers.

**Education**

* Bachelor’s and/or Master’s degree in Civil Engineering, Architecture, Construction Management, or equivalent combination of education and management experience

**Experience**

* **Minimum 15** years of experience as Construction Manager and/or Resident Engineer on multiple simultaneous construction projects
* CCM certification or Professional Engineer (PE) license a plus, although not required
Experience with web based document management systems  
* 

**Take on some of the world’s toughest challenges \- with everyone at GHD backing you every step of the way.**

We'll give you control over your career, empower you to find innovative solutions and help you create a lasting impact.


See where your commitment could take you with GHD.


\#LI\-KM1


Benefits:


* 401K \- Employees are eligible to participate on the first day of the month following 3 months of service
* Paid time off – Our PTO benefit is designed to provide eligible employees with a period of rest and relaxation, sick, and personal time throughout the year. PTO starts at 16 days per year and increases with years of service
* Holiday Pay \- Holiday pay is provided for eligible employee
